Microbiome Research 2023: The human gut microbiome — comprising trillions of microorganisms across thousands of species — plays critical roles in digestion, immune regulation, and neurological function via the gut-brain axis. Recent metagenomic studies have linked microbiome composition to conditions including inflammatory bowel disease, obesity, type 2 diabetes, and depression, opening new avenues for therapeutic intervention. Fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) has demonstrated high efficacy (>90%) for recurrent Clostridioides difficile infection and is being studied for other indications. Personalized nutrition approaches informed by microbiome profiling represent a promising frontier, though causal relationships remain difficult to establish in observational studies.
